paper_title: Re-evaluating Pleistocene-Holocene occupation of cave sites in north-west Thailand

methods.description: Process for obtaining radiocarbon determinations to investigate the hunter-gatherer occupation of north-west Thailand using samples from curated legacy collections.

steps.description: We submitted four freshwater crab (Indochinamon sp.) dactyls from Spirit Cave to the University of Waikato Radiocarbon Dating Laboratory for AMS radiocarbon dating.
steps.searchable_summary: submit samples for AMS radiocarbon dating

steps.description: Twenty-two additional radiocarbon samples, including plant charcoal, human and nonhuman mammalian bone apatite (structural carbonate), freshwater pearl mussel (Margaritifera laosensis) and rice spikelets (Oryza sativa) from Steep Cliff Cave and Banyan Valley Cave were analysed at the University of Georgia Center for Applied Isotope Studies.
steps.searchable_summary: analyze radiocarbon samples at isotope studies center

methods.description: Calculation of the freshwater reservoir correction to account for radiocarbon offsets in freshwater samples.

steps.description: We calculated the freshwater reservoir correction between M. laosensis, Indochinamon sp. and paired charcoal and bone apatite samples.

methods.description: Calibration of radiocarbon determinations using the Bchron package in R and standardization to the Libby 5568-year half-life.

steps.description: Radiocarbon determinations for the figures were analysed and calibrated using the Bchron package in R.
steps.searchable_summary: calibrate radiocarbon dates using Bchron in R

steps.description: All radiocarbon dates are standardised to the Libby 5568-year half-life.
steps.searchable_summary: standardize radiocarbon dates to Libby half-life

methods.description: Analysis of ceramic specimens using optically stimulated luminescence (OSL), thermoluminescence (TL), and infrared stimulated luminescence (IRSL) dating.

steps.description: Four ceramic specimens were analysed by the Luminescence Dating Laboratory at the University of Washington for OSL, TL, and IRSL dating.
steps.searchable_summary: analyze ceramic specimens for luminescence dating
